title: NRI property purchase in India
banner: CA sign-off required on the money trail and tax steps — this list narrows, a CA confirms.

items.title: Fund only through NRE/NRO/FCNR banking channels
items.why: FEMA requires purchase funds to flow through your NRI accounts or inward remittance — cash or resident-account funding creates problems you meet years later at sale/repatriation time.

sources.title: RBI — FEMA, acquisition of immovable property

items.title: Execute a Power of Attorney if buying remotely
items.why: Registration needs presence or a POA — executed at the Indian consulate (or notarized + apostilled), then adjudicated in India. Do it before the deal, not during.

items.title: TDS depends on the SELLER's residency — check both branches
items.why: Buying from a resident: TDS 1% over ₹50L (194-IA). Buying from another NRI: TDS jumps to 20%+ (195) on the whole consideration — buyers who miss this personally owe the shortfall.

items.title: NRI home-loan basics
items.why: NRI loans exist at most large banks: repayment must come from NRE/NRO/inward remittance; expect lower LTV and India-income or co-applicant preferences.

items.title: Know the repatriation limits before you buy
items.why: Sale proceeds repatriation is capped (commonly two residential properties' proceeds; USD 1M/yr overall under LRS-adjacent rules for NRO balances) — if exit-flexibility matters, this shapes what you buy.

items.title: Family gift-funding: document it like an audit is coming
items.why: Gifts from close relatives are tax-exempt but the paper trail (gift deed, donor's source, bank channel) is what protects you — CA sign-off required.

items.title: Stamp duty + registration vary by state (and sometimes by gender)
items.why: Budget 5–8% extra depending on the state; several states discount for women owners — check the state portal for current rates.

items.title: Independent title + encumbrance check
items.why: RERA registration, 30-year title search, encumbrance certificate — the boring paperwork is the entire protection when you live 13,000 km away.
